mentions.note: Enterprise software suffering irrational multiple compression from AI platform fears; no actual earnings misses; money rotating to storage plays; wait for stabilization.
Part of software rotation; up 4.5% today. Group rally may be met with selling after few days of strong performance.
Traditional enterprise software losing pricing power to AI; Anthropic/OpenAI can replicate capabilities cheaper; primacy and supremacy threatened by AI agents + hardware alternatives.
SaaS sector headwind: AI efficiency concerns pressure per-user revenue model.
Part of SaaS cohort that's shrinking. Competition from cheaper design software rising; even after thrashing down, Cramer thinks 'not low enough to own' — more downside likely coming.

CFO quit for Marvell Tech, CEO resigned; quarter disappointing with recurring revenue slowdown; valued at $82B but worth ~$60B max; in secular decline; maybe bounces to $220 but fundamentals broken; get out.
CFO departed for Marvell, CEO already resigned; disappointing quarter; recurring revenue slowdown; secular decline; avoid even on 42% pullback.

Strong Q1: 13% revenue growth, 18% earnings growth; beat guidance; FY raised 12% sales, 16-17% earnings. Firefly ARR tripled, creative premium users 50M→90M. But CFO Durn leaving for Marvell and CEO stepping down create paralyzing leadership void. Stock down 70% from 2021, trades 8.5x earnings despite double-digit growth. Enterprise software hated. Wait for next CEO and CFO before betting on comeback.
Slammed alongside Salesforce and ServiceNow on AI-obsolescence fears for software.
Cited as an example of enterprise software's broad weakness alongside ServiceNow.
Down 7%; part of indiscriminate software sector selloff. Quality company but multiples compressing with no support.
Rally nearly 4% today as big four enterprise software catches bid; vibe coding/anthropic fears lose credibility.
Enterprise software bounce; not going out of business despite AI competition fears.
Creative software incumbent vulnerable to AI-native competitors; natural language and multimodal AI undercut Adobe's traditional complexity and lock-in.
Down 30% Q1 (65% off all-time high, 10x earnings); oscillates between 'dead from AI' and 'great at AI'—reality: users no longer need subscriptions; Figma/Canva cheaper; can always get cheaper.
Software down nearly 4%; dragged down by broader hardware-vs-software rotation.
$7.5B contract notwithstanding, company making no money in market where peers are profitable; rotation away from losers to winners.
Software stock; entire sector hated by market despite company quality.
